task: I’m trying to set up a simple maintenance routine for a couple of things around the house so they don’t turn into bigger problems later. Can you help me sketch out what to check, how often, and what’s worth replacing before it fails?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the easiest way to keep small issues from becoming big ones is to use a **simple routine with a few high-value checks**, then only replace parts when the checks point to real wear.

Here’s a practical framework you can use for most home equipment:

## 1) What to check
Focus on the things that usually fail first:

- **Visual**
  - Leaks
  - Rust/corrosion
  - Loose wires, fittings, mounts
  - Burn marks, discoloration
  - Worn belts, cracked hoses, frayed cords
  - Unusual vibration, noise, smell

- **Basic meter checks** if relevant
  - Voltage at a call/run condition
  - Current draw vs normal
  - Temperature rise vs baseline
  - Continuity of switches/sensors
  - Pressure/flow if it’s a pump, burner, or water system

- **Performance symptoms**
  - Longer run time
  - Short cycling
  - Delayed start
  - Intermittent operation
  - Reduced output

## 2) How often to check
A simple schedule:

- **Monthly:** quick visual walk-through
- **Every 3–6 months:** check the main wear points more carefully
- **Yearly:** full inspection/cleaning and replace known wear items if they’re near end of life
- **After any odd event:** noise, trip, leak, overheating, smell, or performance change

## 3) What’s worth replacing before failure
Replace early when the part is cheap, accessible, and a known wear item:

- Belts
- Filters
- Hoses showing cracks/swelling
- Seals/gaskets with seepage
- Batteries
- Weak capacitors
- Corroded terminals/connectors
- Worn bearings if noise/vibration is repeatable
- Igniters, flame sensors, or switches if they’re showing intermittent faults

## 4) A good decision rule
To avoid chasing noise:

- Log **1 main symptom + 1–2 supporting signs**
- Check them under the **same load/condition**
- Don’t act on a one-off unless it’s severe
- If the same symptom repeats, it becomes real

A simple rule:
- **Electrical/intermittent faults:** act if you get **2 out of 3 repeats under the same load**
- **Mechanical noise/bearing issues:** act if it repeats on **3 cold starts or 3 same-condition runs**
- **Heat issues:** one clear over-baseline reading plus a repeat on the next check is enough

## 5) Simple template you can reuse
For each item, write:

- **Item:**  
- **Check:**  
- **How often:**  
- **Normal reading/condition:**  
- **Replace if:**  
- **Last checked:**  

Example:
- **Sump pump**
  - Check: float movement, run test, discharge flow, noise
  - How often: every 3 months
  - Replace if: float sticks, slow start, loud bearing noise, weak flow
  - Last checked: ___

If you want, I can turn this into a **room-by-room home maintenance checklist** or make it specific for things like **HVAC, sump pump, water heater, furnace, or appliances**.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty generic. Useful, but it’s still hand-wavy.

I’d want this broken into actual categories with intervals that make sense for the failure mode, not just “monthly / yearly” for everything. Like:

- electrical: outlets, breakers, cords, smoke/CO alarms, batteries
- plumbing: under-sink leaks, shutoffs, hoses, water heater, sump pump if there is one
- HVAC: filters, condensate drain, blower noise, thermostat behavior
- appliances: fridge coils, washer hoses, dryer vent, disposal, etc.

And for each one, I want:
- what failure usually looks like first
- what’s cheap enough to replace proactively
- what should be tested under load instead of just eyeballed
- what should be logged so a trend isn’t missed

If you want to make it actually useful, build me a checklist I can print and use, not a general framework.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here’s a **printable, simple maintenance checklist** organized by category, with **check intervals tied to failure modes**, plus **what to replace proactively**, **what to test under load**, and **what to log**.

I’ll keep it tight and practical.

---

# Home Maintenance Checklist
**Goal:** catch wear early without turning this into paperwork.

## Logging rule
For anything that is intermittent or borderline, log just:

- **Date**
- **Item**
- **Symptom**
- **Condition/load**
- **Result**
- **Action**

Example:  
`2026-05-01 | sump pump | loud hum 2 sec before start | full float lift | repeated once | monitor`

If a symptom only shows up after someone has touched it, moved it, or changed the load/ambient conditions, **don’t count that as a real fault yet**.

---

# 1) Electrical

## A. Outlets, switches, cords, plugs
**Check every 6 months**
- Loose plug fit
- Warm outlet faceplate
- Discoloration/burn marks
- Cracked cord jacket
- Plug blades pitted/bent
- Intermittent power when cord is moved

**First failure usually looks like**
- Heat at the outlet or plug
- Flicker/intermittent contact
- Tripping under load
- Scorching or buzzing

**Replace proactively**
- Frayed or stiff cords
- Loose or cracked outlets/switches
- Any plug/cord with heat damage
- Cheap power strips that feel loose or warm

**Test under load**
- Anything that only fails when a device is running: lamp, heater, vacuum, microwave, etc.
- Wiggle test only if the equipment is already plugged in and under normal use, not after moving things around

**Log**
- Heat
- Flicker
- Trip event
- Load type
- Whether it repeats on the same device/outlet

**Trip rule**
- Electrical intermittent fault: **2 of 3 repeats under the same load/condition**

---

## B. Breakers / panel
**Check yearly**
- Any breaker that feels warm
- Signs of corrosion, rust, buzzing, burning smell
- Repeated nuisance trips
- Labels that no longer match reality

**First failure usually looks like**
- Random trips
- Weak/loose breaker handle feel
- Heat at one breaker
- Burn smell or discoloration

**Replace proactively**
- Any breaker showing heat damage or repeated nuisance trips
- Corroded or damaged panel components
- Badly mislabeled circuits if they slow down troubleshooting

**Test under load**
- If a circuit trips only when multiple devices run together, note the combined load

**Log**
- Which breaker
- What was running
- Whether trip was repeatable
- Any heat or smell

---

## C. Smoke / CO alarms
**Check monthly**
- Test button
- Chirp/battery status
- Expiration date
- Mounting condition

**Replace proactively**
- Smoke alarms at end of life, typically **10 years**
- CO alarms per manufacturer, often **5–7 years**
- Any alarm that chirps after fresh battery replacement
- Any unit that fails test button

**Test under load**
- Not really load-based; the test button is enough
- If you have a real-world event like steam, furnace startup, or cooking smoke, note if it false alarms repeatedly

**Log**
- Install date
- Battery replacement date
- Failed test / chirp / false alarm

---

## D. Batteries
**Check every 6 months**
- Smoke/CO alarms
- Flashlights
- UPS units
- Backup systems

**Replace proactively**
- Any battery that fails a test
- Weak batteries in seasonal backup gear
- Batteries past their expected service life

**Log**
- Install date
- Replacement date
- Any low-voltage or chirp event

---

# 2) Plumbing

## A. Under-sink plumbing
**Check every 3 months**
- Drips at traps, supply lines, shutoffs
- Softness/swelling on hoses
- Corrosion at fittings
- Mineral buildup
- Cabinet floor dampness

**First failure usually looks like**
- Small seep at compression fitting
- Slow drip under load
- Shutoff valve seepage when exercised
- Staining or swelling under the sink

**Replace proactively**
- Braided supply lines older than about **10 years**
- Any hose with bulge, crack, corrosion, or stiffness
- Shutoff valves that won’t turn smoothly
- P-trap or gasket showing repeated seepage

**Test under load**
- Run faucet fully and check all joints while water is flowing
- Turn shutoffs on/off gently if they haven’t moved in a while

**Log**
- Leak location
- Whether it only appears during flow
- Valve movement quality

---

## B. Toilets
**Check every 6 months**
- Tank-to-bowl seepage
- Running water
- Weak flush
- Refill valve behavior
- Supply line condition

**First failure usually looks like**
- Toilet runs intermittently
- Flapper seals poorly
- Fill valve gets noisy or slow
- Supply line sweating or corrosion

**Replace proactively**
- Flapper if it starts leaking or hardening
- Fill valve if it becomes noisy, slow, or inconsistent
- Supply line if old or corroded

**Test under load**
- Flush several times in a row
- Watch for delayed refill or running after flush

**Log**
- Run time after flush
- Refill noise
- Any repeat running

---

## C. Water heater
**Check every 6 months**
- Leaks at base, T&P valve, fittings
- Rust at connections
- Rumbling/popping noise
- Water temperature consistency
- Anode/service if you track it

**First failure usually looks like**
- Sediment noise
- Slow hot water recovery
- Small seep at valve/fitting
- Rust at top or base

**Replace proactively**
- T&P valve if it drips or won’t test properly
- Flexible connectors if corroded
- Anode rod if you’re servicing on schedule
- Heater itself if tank rust/leak starts

**Test under load**
- Check temperature and recovery during normal hot water use
- Watch for repeated relief valve discharge or unusual burner/heating cycle behavior

**Log**
- Hot water recovery time
- Any leak
- Any rumbling/popping
- Temperature changes

---

## D. Sump pump, if present
**Check every 3 months, and after heavy rain**
- Float movement
- Pump start/stop
- Discharge flow
- Check valve behavior
- Noise/vibration
- Pit debris

**First failure usually looks like**
- Float sticking
- Delayed start
- Loud motor hum
- Weak discharge
- Short cycling

**Replace proactively**
- Float switch if it sticks or is intermittent
- Check valve if backflow causes repeat cycling
- Pump if bearings get noisy, starts slowly, or flow drops
- Backup battery if applicable and weak

**Test under load**
- Full float-lift run test
- Check discharge during actual pumping
- Repeat under the same water level if possible

**Log**
- Start delay
- Noise
- Flow strength
- Repeatability of any failure

**Trip rule**
- Mechanical noise: **3 cold starts or 3 same-condition runs**
- Electrical intermittent fault: **2 of 3 under same load**

---

# 3) HVAC

## A. Air filters
**Check monthly**
- Dirt loading
- Airflow restriction
- Visible collapse or bypass

**First failure usually looks like**
- Reduced airflow
- Longer run times
- Higher noise from blower
- Dust buildup around registers

**Replace proactively**
- Filters when visibly loaded or on schedule
- Cheap filters before they become restrictive

**Test under load**
- Compare airflow and system run time before/after filter change

**Log**
- Install date
- Replace date
- Any airflow change

---

## B. Condensate drain / pan
**Check every 3 months in cooling season**
- Standing water
- Slime/algae
- Clog signs
- Overflow switch activity

**First failure usually looks like**
- Water in pan
- Overflow shutoff
- Musty smell
- Intermittent AC shutdown

**Replace proactively**
- Drain tubing if brittle or clogged repeatedly
- Float switch if it sticks
- Pan if corroded/cracked

**Test under load**
- Run AC long enough to produce condensate
- Verify drainage while system is actually cooling

**Log**
- Any overflow
- Drain speed
- Musty smell
- Shutdown event

---

## C. Blower / fan / outdoor unit noise
**Check every 6 months**
- Vibration
- Bearing noise
- Rattle
- Startup delay
- Short cycling

**First failure usually looks like**
- New hum, squeal, scrape, or rattle
- Fan starts slower than usual
- Intermittent shutdowns

**Replace proactively**
- Capacitor if weak signs show up
- Fan motor if noise repeats under same condition
- Mounts if vibration is the issue

**Test under load**
- Same temperature setting, same cycle state, same fan mode
- Listen at startup and steady run

**Log**
- Noise type
- Startup delay
- Same-condition repeat count

**Trip rule**
- Mechanical bearing/gearbox type noise: **3 cold starts or 3 same-load runs**
- Electrical intermittent fault: **2 of 3 under same load**

---

## D. Thermostat behavior
**Check yearly**
- Temperature swings
- Delayed response
- Short cycling
- Wrong readings vs room temperature

**First failure usually looks like**
- System overshoots
- Doesn’t call for heat/cool reliably
- Display glitches or battery issues

**Replace proactively**
- Weak thermostat batteries
- Old thermostat with unstable control
- Units with repeat control errors

**Test under load**
- Compare setpoint vs actual room temp during a normal heating/cooling cycle

**Log**
- Overshoot
- Short cycling
- Missed calls
- Battery changes

---

# 4) Appliances

## A. Refrigerator
**Check every 6 months**
- Coil dust
- Door seal condition
- Compressor noise
- Temperature stability
- Frost buildup

**First failure usually looks like**
- Longer compressor run time
- Warm spots
- Door seal leaks
- Fan noise or clicking

**Replace proactively**
- Door gaskets if they don’t seal
- Water filters on schedule
- Fan or relay parts if noise/intermittent start shows up

**Test under load**
- Check temperature after normal use, not right after opening the door repeatedly
- Listen for repeated startup issues

**Log**
- Cycle length
- Temp stability
- Noise
- Frost or seal issue

---

## B. Washer
**Check every 3 months**
- Supply hose condition
- Drain hose condition
- Leaks at fill/drain
- Drum vibration
- Cycle completion

**First failure usually looks like**
- Slow fill
- Drain problems
- Excess vibration
- Hose bulge or seepage

**Replace proactively**
- Rubber supply hoses; braided ones on age/condition
- Any hose with cracking, bulging, or rusted clamps
- Worn door seals or tub seals if leaking

**Test under load**
- Run a normal cycle with a real load
- Watch fill, agitate, drain, spin

**Log**
- Leak location
- Vibration
- Cycle errors
- Drain speed

---

## C. Dryer
**Check every 3 months**
- Lint buildup
- Vent flow
- Drum noise
- Heating consistency
- Burn smell

**First failure usually looks like**
- Longer drying time
- Hot exterior
- Weak vent flow
- Squeal or rumble
- Thermal cutout behavior

**Replace proactively**
- Dryer vent hose if crushed or lint-clogged
- Drum belt if slipping/noisy
- Heating components if cycling abnormally
- Lint screen if damaged

**Test under load**
- Run a normal load and check exhaust flow and dry time

**Log**
- Dry time
- Exhaust heat/flow
- Noise
- Any overheating

---

## D. Dishwasher
**Check every 6 months**
- Door seal
- Spray arm movement
- Drain performance
- Filter cleanliness
- Leak under cabinet

**First failure usually looks like**
- Poor cleaning
- Standing water
- Leaks at door or hose
- Pump noise

**Replace proactively**
- Door gasket if leaking
- Drain hose if brittle/kinked
- Circulation pump parts if noise repeats

**Test under load**
- Run a normal cycle
- Check for leaks and full drain

**Log**
- Cleaning performance
- Drain speed
- Leak location
- Noise

---

## E. Garbage disposal
**Check every 6 months**
- Noise
- Slow grinding
- Reset trips
- Leaks under sink

**First failure usually looks like**
- Humming without turning
- Frequent resets
- Leaks at bottom seal
- Grinding gets weaker

**Replace proactively**
- Unit if it hums, binds, or leaks repeatedly
- Mounting gasket if seepage starts

**Test under load**
- Run with water and normal food waste load
- Note if it starts slowly or trips

**Log**
- Start quality
- Trip/reset count
- Leak

---

# 5) Simple replacement thresholds
Use these as “replace before it becomes a bigger problem” rules:

- **Any cord, hose, or seal with visible cracking, bulging, or heat damage**
- **Any part that fails repeatably under the same condition**
- **Any intermittent electrical fault that hits 2 of 3 times**
- **Any bearing/mechanical noise that repeats on 3 cold starts or 3 same-load runs**
- **Any heat issue with one clear over-baseline reading plus a repeat on the next check**
- **Any item that leaks more than once after tightening or reseating**
- **Any cheap wear item that’s old enough to be near expected life and is already showing symptoms**

---

# 6) One-page print version
If you want the shortest usable version, use this:

## Monthly
- Smoke/CO alarms test
- Quick visual for leaks, scorch marks, loose cords, unusual noise
- Check HVAC filter
- Check under sinks for dampness

## Every 3 months
- Under-sink plumbing
- Washer hoses
- Dryer vent/lint
- Sump pump test if present
- Condensate drain in cooling season

## Every 6 months
- Outlets/cords/switches
- Refrigerator coils and seals
- Dishwasher/garbage disposal
- Toilet mechanisms
- Water heater leak/noise check

## Yearly
- Breaker panel
- Thermostat behavior
- Full appliance inspection
- Replace age-based wear items

---

If you want, I can turn this into a **clean checkbox sheet** with blank lines you can print, or make a **version just for the exact items you own**.

user_simulator.text:
Better, but still a little broad in a few spots.

I want the printable version to be tighter and more conditional, not just a big list with intervals. Couple things:

- separate **by failure mode** where it matters — heat, intermittent electrical, leaks, airflow restriction, mechanical wear
- give me **actual replacement triggers** instead of “if symptoms show up”
- call out **what should be powered / loaded / running** when checked, because eyeballing a dead system is half useless
- and don’t bury the logging part. I want a short log field for each item, not one generic rule at the top

Also, some of the intervals feel a bit soft. For example, smoke/CO alarms and hoses are not in the same bucket as a breaker panel or a thermostat. I’d rather see something like:

- check frequency
- test condition
- replace criteria
- note to log

per item, in a table or checklist format.

If you can, strip out the explanatory paragraphs and just give me a print-ready sheet.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **print-ready, tighter checklist** organized by **failure mode**, with:

- **check frequency**
- **test condition** (powered / loaded / running)
- **replacement criteria**
- **what to log**

I’ve kept it as a sheet you can actually use.

---

# Home Maintenance Checklist
**Use this as a print sheet.**  
**Log format:** `Date | item | condition/load | symptom | result | action`

---

## 1) ELECTRICAL

| Item | Check frequency | Test condition | Replace criteria | Log |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Smoke alarms** | Monthly | Press test button; verify alarm sounds | Replace at **10 years** from manufacture; replace if chirp continues after fresh battery; replace if test fails | Install date, test result, chirp date, battery date |
| **CO alarms** | Monthly | Press test button; verify alarm sounds | Replace at **5–7 years** or per label; replace if test fails or chirps after battery change | Install date, test result, chirp date |
| **Outlet / plug / cord** | Every 6 months | Check **under normal load**: lamp, heater, vacuum, etc. | Replace if warm, discolored, loose, cracked, arcing, or intermittent under same load **2 of 3 times** | Outlet, load used, heat/flicker, repeat count |
| **Power strip / surge protector** | Every 6 months | Use with normal devices plugged in | Replace if loose outlets, heat, scorch marks, damaged cord, or breaker/trip behavior repeats | Device, load, heat, trip, age |
| **Breaker / panel** | Yearly | Observe during normal household load; note any nuisance trip | Replace/repair if repeated trip on same circuit, heat, smell, corrosion, or damaged breaker handle | Circuit, what was running, trip count, heat/smell |
| **Batteries in alarms / backup gear** | Every 6 months | Test in device under normal use | Replace if low battery, failed test, or past service life | Device, battery date, result |

### Electrical replacement triggers
- **Intermittent fault:** repeat **2 of 3** under same load/condition
- **Heat issue:** one clear hot spot + repeat on next check
- **Visible damage:** replace immediately

---

## 2) PLUMBING

| Item | Check frequency | Test condition | Replace criteria | Log |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Under-sink supply lines** | Every 3 months | Run faucet fully; check while water is flowing | Replace if bulged, cracked, corroded, stiff, damp, or seep repeats after tightening | Location, flow condition, leak point, age |
| **Under-sink shutoff valves** | Every 6 months | Exercise valve gently; check under flow | Replace if stiff, seeping, won’t fully close, or leaks after exercise | Valve, movement, seepage |
| **Under-sink drain / trap** | Every 3 months | Run sink full flow; inspect joints while draining | Replace if drip repeats, gasket won’t seal, or staining returns after reseat | Joint, flow state, drip repeat |
| **Toilet flapper / fill valve** | Every 6 months | Flush 3 times in a row; observe refill | Replace flapper if tank leaks down; replace fill valve if noisy, slow, or inconsistent | Flush count, refill time, noise |
| **Toilet supply line** | Every 6 months | Inspect under normal pressure | Replace if corrosion, bulge, cracking, or seepage | Line age, condition |
| **Water heater** | Every 6 months | Check while unit is heating / after hot water use | Replace T&P valve if it drips or fails test; replace connectors if corroded; replace tank if rust or base leak appears | Leak, recovery time, noise, temp |
| **Sump pump** | Every 3 months + after heavy rain | **Lift float and run pump**; verify discharge flow | Replace if float sticks, pump hums without starting, starts slowly, weak discharge, or short-cycles | Start delay, flow, noise, repeat count |

### Plumbing replacement triggers
- **Leak repeats after one tighten/reseat**
- **Any hose with bulge/crack/corrosion**
- **Any valve that won’t move smoothly**
- **Any pump noise that repeats on 3 same-condition runs**
- **Any electrical/mechanical fault on sump pump that repeats 2 of 3 times**

---

## 3) HVAC

| Item | Check frequency | Test condition | Replace criteria | Log |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Air filter** | Monthly | Check while system is running or just after | Replace when visibly loaded, airflow drops, or per filter rating | Install date, dirt level, airflow feel |
| **Return / supply airflow** | Monthly | HVAC running | Investigate if airflow weak, uneven, or system runs longer than usual | Room, airflow, run time |
| **Condensate drain / pan** | Every 3 months in cooling season | Run AC long enough to produce condensate | Clear/repair if standing water, overflow, slime, musty smell, or shutdown occurs | Water present, smell, shutdown |
| **Blower / fan / outdoor unit noise** | Every 6 months | Check **while running** and at startup | Replace capacitor/motor if startup delay, squeal, scrape, hum, or rattle repeats on **3 same-load runs** | Noise type, startup delay, repeat count |
| **Thermostat** | Yearly | Compare setpoint to actual room temp during a normal cycle | Replace if display glitches, misses calls, short-cycles, or temp control drifts repeatedly | Setpoint, actual temp, cycle behavior |

### HVAC replacement triggers
- **Mechanical noise:** repeat on **3 cold starts or 3 same-load runs**
- **Electrical intermittent fault:** repeat **2 of 3**
- **Condensate issue:** any overflow or shutdown event
- **Filter:** replace before restriction causes longer run times

---

## 4) APPLIANCES

| Item | Check frequency | Test condition | Replace criteria | Log |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Refrigerator door gasket** | Every 6 months | Door closed; check seal with running compressor if possible | Replace if gasket is torn, hard, warped, or leaks cold air | Seal condition, temp stability |
| **Refrigerator coils / fan noise** | Every 6 months | Unit running | Clean coils if dusty; replace fan/relay if startup issues or repeat noise | Noise, cycle length, frost |
| **Washer supply hoses** | Every 3 months | Under water pressure / during fill | Replace if rubber hose is older, cracked, bulged, or any seepage appears | Hose age, fill leak, condition |
| **Washer drain hose** | Every 3 months | During drain/spin | Replace if kinked, cracked, loose, or leaking under drain load | Drain behavior, leak point |
| **Washer vibration / cycle completion** | Every 3 months | Run a normal load | Investigate if shaking, walking, error codes, or incomplete cycle repeats | Load type, vibration, error |
| **Dryer vent / hose** | Every 3 months | Dryer running with normal load | Replace if crushed, disconnected, lint-packed, or exhaust flow is weak | Dry time, exhaust flow, heat |
| **Dryer drum / belt noise** | Every 3 months | Run with normal load | Replace belt/idler if squeal, rumble, or scrape repeats on **3 same-load runs** | Noise, dry time, repeat count |
| **Dishwasher door seal** | Every 6 months | Run a normal cycle | Replace if leak appears at door, gasket is torn/hard, or seep repeats | Leak location, cycle stage |
| **Dishwasher drain / pump** | Every 6 months | Full cycle with normal load | Replace if standing water remains, pump noise repeats, or drain is slow | Drain time, noise, residue |
| **Garbage disposal** | Every 6 months | Run with water and normal food load | Replace if hums without turning, trips repeatedly, leaks, or grinds weakly | Start quality, trip count, leak |

### Appliance replacement triggers
- **Leak repeats once after reseat/tighten**
- **Mechanical noise repeats on 3 same-load runs**
- **Electrical intermittent fault repeats 2 of 3**
- **Any unit that hums but won’t start under normal load**
- **Any hose/cord/seal with visible damage**

---

# SHORT DAILY / MONTHLY ROUTINE

## Monthly
- Smoke alarms test
- CO alarms test
- Look for leaks under sinks
- Check HVAC filter
- Listen for new noise while systems are running

## Every 3 months
- Under-sink supply/drain lines
- Washer hoses
- Dryer vent/hose
- Sump pump test if present
- Condensate drain in cooling season

## Every 6 months
- Outlets / cords / power strips under load
- Refrigerator gasket/coils
- Dishwasher seal/drain
- Toilet fill/flapper behavior
- Water heater leak/noise check

## Yearly
- Breaker panel
- Thermostat behavior
- Replace age-based alarms/batteries as needed

---

# QUICK LOG FIELDS
Use one line per item:

`Date | Item | Condition/load | Symptom | Repeat? | Action`

Examples:
- `2026-05-01 | sump pump | float lift | hum 2 sec before start | yes | monitor`
- `2026-05-01 | outlet | vacuum running | warm faceplate | yes | replace`
- `2026-05-01 | washer hose | fill cycle | damp at crimp | yes | replace`

---

If you want, I can turn this into a **clean one-page checkbox sheet** next, with blank lines and less text so it’s easier to print and keep on the fridge.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s much closer. Tight enough to use. The failure-mode split and the loaded test condition are the part I wanted, not the generic fluff.

The log lines are fine too. I can work with that.
